title: Ebola Crisis in the DRC: A Difficult Fight to Save Lives
subtitle: As the outbreak continues to grow, health workers face shortages and community fear in the Ituri province.

summary.en: The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo has reached a critical point one month after it began. With hundreds of cases reported, medical teams are struggling to manage the virus while facing equipment shortages. Local communities are also showing resistance to health measures due to misinformation. International experts emphasize that flexible funding is necessary to stop the spread. Meanwhile, neighboring countries like Uganda are working hard to keep the situation under control.
summary.zh: 剛果民主共和國的伊波拉疫情在爆發一個月後已來到關鍵時刻。隨著數百起病例通報，醫療團隊在面臨設備短缺的同時，正艱難地控制病毒擴散。由於錯誤資訊的影響，當地社區也對防疫措施表現出抗拒。國際專家強調，靈活的資金對於遏止疫情擴散至關重要。與此同時，烏干達等鄰國正努力將疫情控制在掌握之中。

paragraphs.en: One month after the latest Ebola outbreak began in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), the situation remains deeply concerning. The virus, known as the Bundibugyo virus, is spreading quickly. According to The Guardian Health, the number of victims is roughly doubling every week. This crisis has touched many lives, including a tragic incident at an orphanage in Bunia. After a newborn baby died from the virus, four nuns who cared for the child also became sick. This event shows the dangerous reality for those on the front lines of the fight against the disease.
paragraphs.zh: 在剛果民主共和國（Democratic Republic of the Congo, DRC）爆發最新一波伊波拉疫情滿一個月後，情況依然令人深感憂心。這種被稱為邦迪布焦病毒（Bundibugyo virus）的病毒正迅速擴散。根據《衛報健康》（The Guardian Health）報導，受害者人數大約每週翻倍。這場危機影響了許多人的生命，包括在布尼亞（Bunia）一家孤兒院發生的悲劇。一名新生兒因感染該病毒死亡後，照顧該嬰兒的四名修女也隨之染病。此事件凸顯了站在防疫第一線人員所面臨的危險現實。

paragraphs.en: Latest figures from the United Nations show 676 confirmed cases and 136 deaths. Most of these cases are in the Ituri province of the DRC. While neighboring Uganda has reported 19 cases and two deaths, health officials there say the situation is under control thanks to careful tracking of people who may have been exposed to the virus. Despite the risk to the rest of the world remaining low, 22 countries have already placed travel restrictions on people arriving from the DRC, Uganda, or South Sudan. Africa CDC, the health agency of the African Union, notes that these travel bans have made it harder for health organizations to get the help they need to the right places.
paragraphs.zh: 聯合國的最新數據顯示，目前已有六百七十六起確診病例，並造成一百三十六人死亡。這些病例大多集中在剛果民主共和國的伊圖里省（Ituri province）。儘管鄰國烏干達通報了十九起病例與兩起死亡案例，但當地的衛生官員表示，由於對可能接觸病毒的人員進行了嚴謹的追蹤，目前情況仍在控制之中。儘管對世界其他地區的風險仍然偏低，但已有二十二個國家對來自剛果民主共和國、烏干達或南蘇丹的旅客實施了旅遊限制。非洲疾病管制暨預防中心（Africa CDC），即非洲聯盟的衛生機構指出，這些旅遊禁令使得衛生組織更難將所需的援助送達正確的地點。

paragraphs.en: Frontline medical workers are facing many challenges. They report a lack of basic supplies, such as personal protective equipment—special clothing and gear used to keep doctors and nurses safe from infection—and vehicles to transport the bodies of those who have passed away. While testing has improved, experts say it must be faster and performed closer to where patients are staying. Dr. Babou Rukengeza, who leads the Ebola response for the group Save the Children, described the situation as truly devastating. All staff and children at the affected orphanage are now being watched closely for any signs of illness.
paragraphs.zh: 第一線的醫療人員正面臨許多挑戰。他們回報缺乏基本物資，例如個人防護裝備（即用於保護醫師與護理師免受感染的特殊衣物與裝備），以及運送遺體的車輛。雖然檢測能力有所提升，但專家表示檢測速度必須加快，且應在更靠近病患居住地的地方進行。負責「救助兒童會」（Save the Children）伊波拉應對工作的巴布·魯肯格扎醫師（Dr. Babou Rukengeza）形容目前的情況確實令人痛心。受影響孤兒院的所有工作人員與兒童，目前正受到密切監控，以觀察是否有任何染病跡象。

paragraphs.en: Beyond the lack of supplies, there are other major problems slowing down the response. Conflict continues in the areas hit hardest by the virus, making it dangerous for aid workers to travel. Additionally, misinformation is spreading rapidly. Some people are avoiding hospitals because they do not trust the care provided, and there have even been violent attacks on treatment centers and the people who work there. In some markets, people have been seen denying that the virus exists at all, sometimes blaming Red Cross workers for the spread of the disease. Africa CDC has called this lack of community trust a critical challenge for those trying to stop the outbreak.
paragraphs.zh: 除了物資匱乏之外，還有其他嚴重問題拖慢了應對進度。病毒重災區持續存在衝突，使得援助人員的移動變得危險。此外，錯誤資訊正迅速傳播。有些人因為不信任醫療照護而避開醫院，甚至發生了針對治療中心及其工作人員的暴力攻擊事件。在一些市場中，有人公然否認病毒的存在，甚至將疫情擴散歸咎於紅十字會的工作人員。非洲疾病管制暨預防中心將這種社區信任的缺失，視為防疫工作面臨的關鍵挑戰。

paragraphs.en: Local leaders are also speaking out about the slow pace of help. Gratien Iracan, a member of parliament for Bunia, noted on social media that despite promises of millions of dollars from the international community, these resources are not yet visible on the ground. He shared stories of doctors who died while complaining about poor care and communities that called for help but never received it. This has led to feelings of anger and confusion among the local people who are trying to survive the crisis.
paragraphs.zh: 地方領袖也對援助進度緩慢表達不滿。布尼亞的國會議員格拉蒂安·伊拉坎（Gratien Iracan）在社群媒體上指出，儘管國際社會承諾提供數百萬美元的援助，但這些資源在當地尚未見蹤影。他分享了醫師在抱怨醫療資源匱乏中過世的故事，以及社區求助卻始終未獲回應的情況。這導致當地試圖在危機中求生存的民眾，感到憤怒與困惑。

paragraphs.en: Despite these difficult conditions, there are reasons to remain hopeful. Scientists are working quickly to test and create vaccines, and new research suggests that existing medicines used to fight viruses may be helpful. Organizations involved in the response are meeting every day to coordinate their plans, and African leaders are scheduled to meet to discuss funding. Experts continue to stress that what the DRC needs most is flexible funding—money that can be used quickly for whatever is needed most at that moment. As the world watches, the success of the response will depend on whether these resources can reach the people in Ituri who need them most, and whether the medical teams can build the trust necessary to provide life-saving care.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管環境艱困，仍有理由保持希望。科學家正加緊腳步測試並研發疫苗，而新研究顯示，現有的抗病毒藥物或許能發揮作用。參與應對工作的組織每天開會協調計畫，非洲各國領袖也預計將召開會議討論資金問題。專家持續強調，剛果民主共和國最需要的是靈活運用的資金，即能迅速用於當下最迫切需求的款項。在全世界的關注下，防疫工作的成功與否，將取決於這些資源能否送達伊圖里最需要的人手中，以及醫療團隊能否建立起提供救命照護所必需的信任。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To affect or influence someone's life, often in a negative or emotional way.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 影響、波及（通常指負面或令人感傷的影響）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「觸摸」，這裡指事件對人們生活的影響。文中：This crisis has touched many lives, including a tragic incident at an orphanage in Bunia.

keyTerms.definitionEn: In the actual location where events are happening, rather than at a distance or in a planning office.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 在現場、在實際執行的地方。
keyTerms.noteZh: 字面意思為「在地上」，這裡指實際的執行現場。文中：He shared stories of doctors who died while complaining about poor care and communities that called for help but never received it.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To state one's opinion publicly, especially in protest or to demand change.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 公開發表意見、大聲疾呼（通常指為了抗議或要求改變）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「說話」，這裡指公開表達立場。文中：Local leaders are also speaking out about the slow pace of help.

sources.name: The Guardian Health
sources.title: Ebola one month on: will the latest outbreak in DRC become the most deadly yet?

previousArticles.title: Tragedy in Kenya: Mother Finds Son After Ebola Center Protests
previousArticles.summaryEn: A mother in Nanyuki, Kenya, has discovered the body of her 17-year-old son following violent protests against a planned US Ebola quarantine center. The teenager was caught in clashes between police and demonstrators who fear the facility poses public health risks. While the government defends the project as a humanitarian necessity, local groups argue that the government has lacked transparency. Experts note that successful disease response requires working closely with local communities rather than ignoring their concerns. The incident marks the third death linked to the ongoing unrest surrounding the construction site.

previousArticles.title: Measles Outbreak in England Leads to Two Child Deaths
previousArticles.summaryEn: Two children in England have died from measles this year, marking a tragic turn in the current health crisis. Health authorities report over 100 new infections in the last two weeks alone. Most of those affected are unvaccinated children aged 10 and under. Experts warn that the disease is spreading easily and can lead to serious complications. Officials are now calling for parents to ensure their children receive the necessary MMR vaccines.

previousArticles.title: Health Experts Warn of Growing Ebola Risks in the Democratic Republic of Congo
previousArticles.summaryEn: The Democratic Republic of Congo is facing a difficult Ebola outbreak that is worrying global health officials. Medical workers are struggling to contain the virus while lacking basic safety equipment. Experts warn that the current situation puts both patients and doctors at high risk. Recent reports highlight the fear and uncertainty felt by those living in affected areas. International health organizations are calling for more support to help stop the spread of the disease.

quiz.question: What specific challenge regarding medical supplies is mentioned in the third paragraph?

options.A: A shortage of vaccines for the local population.
options.B: A lack of protective gear and vehicles for transporting the deceased.
options.C: The inability to find enough doctors and nurses to staff the orphanages.
options.D: The high cost of testing equipment that is currently unavailable.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文章第三段明確指出前線醫護人員面臨缺乏個人防護裝備（PPE）以及運送遺體的車輛。選項 A 雖然提到疫苗研發，但並非該段落強調的「基本物資短缺」；選項 C 提到醫護人員短缺，但文中並未明確指出是「找不到」人；選項 D 提到檢測需加速，但並未說檢測設備買不起，因此 B 最符合文意。

quiz.question: Based on the article, why might the travel restrictions imposed by 22 countries be considered counterproductive?

options.A: They have caused the virus to spread more rapidly to neighboring countries like Uganda.
options.B: They have led to a decrease in the number of confirmed cases reported by the UN.
options.C: They prevent necessary aid and resources from reaching the affected regions efficiently.
options.D: They have caused local leaders in Bunia to stop trusting international organizations.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第二段，Africa CDC 指出這些旅遊禁令使得衛生組織更難將所需的援助送達正確的地點，這意味著禁令反而阻礙了救援行動。選項 A 文中未提及禁令導致擴散；選項 B 與文中事實不符；選項 D 提到當地領袖的不信任，但這主要源於資源未到位，而非旅遊禁令本身，故 C 為最合理的推論。

quiz.question: In the fourth paragraph, what does the phrase 'critical challenge' mean in the context of the Ebola response?

options.A: A problem that is extremely important and needs to be solved to ensure success.
options.B: A dangerous situation involving physical violence against medical staff.
options.C: A negative judgment made by the community about the quality of hospital care.
options.D: A technical difficulty related to the speed of medical testing procedures.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。在此上下文中，critical 指的是「關鍵的」或「決定性的」，指該問題（缺乏社區信任）對於防疫工作至關重要，若不解決便難以成功。選項 B 雖然文中提到暴力事件，但「critical challenge」指的是整體挑戰的性質；選項 C 僅是該挑戰的一個面向；選項 D 與檢測速度相關，並非該詞彙在此處的定義，故 A 最為精確。

quiz.question: What is the primary message of the article regarding the Ebola outbreak in the DRC?

options.A: The outbreak is primarily caused by local misinformation and distrust of medical workers.
options.B: International travel bans are the most effective way to contain the spread of the virus.
options.C: The response to the outbreak is hindered by complex logistical, social, and political issues.
options.D: The situation is improving rapidly due to new vaccines and increased international funding.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章從多個角度探討了疫情：物資缺乏（第三段）、衝突與誤解（第四段）、資源分配不均（第五段）以及對未來的展望（第六段）。這顯示出防疫工作面臨多重障礙。選項 A 只涵蓋了部分原因；選項 B 與文中觀點相反；選項 D 過於樂觀，文中提到資源尚未到位，故 C 正確總結了文章核心。
